sections.label: Lyrics
lines: I saw a hill bathed in dawn’s first light,
Where war forgot its name.
No flags to burn, no borders drawn,
No echo left of blame.
The earth exhaled, and in that breath,
The old knives turned to rust —
They planted trees where walls once stood,
And raised their hope from dust.
We’ve walked the fire, we’ve drunk the storm,
Now silence sings again —
A river flows from sacred stone,
And peace begins within.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the night, not the sword, not the grave.
Under vine and fig, we lay unarmed,
In the hush of a world remade.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the roar, not the wound, not the flame.
From the mountain of peace, we rise and say:
We are home, we are whole, we are brave.
Children dance where tanks once crawled,
Their laughter floods the sky.
The lion lays beside the lamb,
No blood beneath their cry.
The widow sings, the prisoner dreams,
The lost have found their name —
And every tongue in harmony
Now calls the earth reclaimed.
The swords we bore, now garden tools —
We cut the soil, not skin.
The only scars that heaven keeps
Are the ones that let light in.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the night, not the sword, not the grave.
Under vine and fig, we lay unarmed,
In the hush of a world remade.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the past, not the chain, not the shame.
From the mountain of peace, we rise and say:
We are found, we are flame, we are grace.
Swords into plowshares
Spears into pruning hooks.
No more training for war.
No more hunger for more.
Just the still of enough —
And the heart made pure.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the dark, not the doubt, not the days.
Under vine and fig, we lay unarmed,
Where the Prince of Peace still reigns.
And none shall make us afraid —
Not the storm, not the sting, not the grave.
From the mountain of light, we rise and say:
We are home, we are whole, we are brave.
